text: Marcus Aurelius practiced a meditation he called ekpyrosis — imagining the periodic Stoic conflagration in which the cosmos collapses and renews. He applied this not to cultivate despair but to test the weight of his concerns: would this controversy survive the fire? The technique asks you to project forward to a moment when your current distraction will be utterly gone, then work backward. In that light, the notification, the slight, the argument about credit — they fail the test immediately. Only work that adds to virtue or serves others survives the ekpyrosis thought experiment with any dignity.
question: What is the purpose of Marcus Aurelius's ekpyrosis meditation as described in the passage?

options.text: To prepare emotionally for inevitable personal loss and grief
To test the weight of concerns by projecting to a moment when they will be completely gone
To achieve a trance state that suppresses the default mode network
To rehearse Stoic cosmology as a form of intellectual discipline
